ip_outcome_note: Confirmed via PokemonTCG.io swsh4-24. National Pokedex no. 5 matches. Orange fire-lizard with flaming tail and claws is canonical Charmeleon design. Wave 32 previously verified charizard-line character node connection.

trivia_bullets.text: Charmeleon appears in Sword & Shield — Vivid Voltage (2020) as no. 024/185, an Uncommon. National Pokedex no. 5, mid-stage of the Charmander line, one of the original three Kanto starter evolution lines.

Third Charmeleon print in the corpus. VIV-024 art by SATOSHI NAKAI presents a close mid-shot with dark fiery background and strong rim lighting — more aggressive, close-action register compared to other prints (e.g. BUS-19).
"It has a barbaric nature. In battle, it whips its fiery tail around and slashes away with sharp claws."
Raging Flames (FireFire, 60 damage) costs discarding the top 3 cards of your deck — a self-destructive mechanic consistent with Charmeleon's lore of barely-controlled aggression.

Wired to the charizard-line character node (wave 32). Charmeleon's Pokedex entries describe it as difficult to control and attacking anyone who provokes it regardless of trainer bond — behavior that carries into Charizard's disobedience arc in the anime.
